fragile.label: Fragile
fragile.desc: Positional / structural - breaks on layout changes.

context.label: Context
context.desc: Text / class / attribute - depends on your project.

dynamic.label: Dynamic
dynamic.desc: Built at runtime - not classified (blind spot).

intro: Audit the locators in your Playwright/TypeScript tests - fully in your browser, nothing leaves the page. Point it at a folder, or paste a single file.
needChromium: Folder picking needs a Chromium browser (Chrome / Edge). You can still paste a file below.
nothingToAnalyze: Nothing to analyze - pick a folder or paste some test code.
noLocatorsDesc: This doesn't look like a Playwright/TS test suite - there's simply nothing to analyze here. That's not a verdict.
coverage: {{files}} files · {{calls}} locator calls · classified {{classified}} · dynamic {{dynamic}} (not classified)
noFragile: No fragile locators in this pass. Good sign - but a first pass, not a full verdict (tests not run).
honesty: LocateMe reads test code statically and groups locators by shape. It explains why, never auto-fixes, and gives no single score. First pass, not a full verdict - tests are not executed.
copiesTip: This exact selector appears {{count}} times in the suite.
sortFileHint: Grouped by file, top to bottom
sortHotHint: Files with the most fragile locators
emptyTitle: Point LocateMe at your Playwright tests.
emptyDesc: Reads locator shapes statically. No code runs, nothing leaves your browser.
selectHint: Select a fragile locator on the left to inspect it here.

explain.fragile: It points at a position or structure in the page - an index, :nth-child, an auto-generated class, or a bare path. When the markup or order changes (even with no real bug) the locator stops matching and the test fails. Prefer role, test-id, or label.
explain.stable: It uses role, test-id, or label - tied to intent, not layout. It survives markup changes and is the recommended default.
explain.context: It matches by visible text, class, or a project attribute. Fine while those are stable, but it can break on localization, copy edits, or styling refactors.
explain.dynamic: Its selector is built at runtime (a variable or template), so the final value isn't visible in the code. We can't classify it - a blind spot worth a manual look.

about.p1: LocateMe is a static locator-fragility audit for Playwright / TypeScript test suites. It reads your test code only - no runtime, no test execution - and groups locators by shape: fragile, stable, context, or dynamic.
about.p2: It surfaces why a locator may be brittle and where the same selector repeats, so you can fix one place and close many. It never auto-rewrites your tests and gives no single quality score - just facts you can act on.
about.p3: Everything runs in your browser. Your code never leaves the page. Open source, MIT.
